title: fritillaria nigra subsp. boissieri
intro: Fritillaria nigra subsp. boissieri, commonly known as the black lily of the valley, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family, native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and Greece. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Fritillaria nigra subsp. boissieri include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its use in traditional Chinese medicine to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tincture, tea, or dried bulb powder, and is also used to make injectable preparations for the treatment of certain respiratory conditions.
